article_meta.title: Politics Trump signs dozens of executive or

reasoning: The summary is largely more neutral than the source, avoiding the article’s charged phrasing. Minor lapses include mildly evaluative wording (e.g., “swift and assertive”) and using “illegal immigration.” Major perspectives weren’t distorted, but the sources_represented field omits the administration/Trump perspective present in the article.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses mildly interpretive phrasing like “swift and assertive start,” though most verbs are neutral.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States “reductions in government spending,” which isn’t directly supported; otherwise actions are factual and from the article.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: “Assertive” is an evaluative descriptor not attributed; otherwise neutral.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No alarmist or dramatic language; tone remains measured.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions beyond paraphrasing the article’s stated goals.
custom_reasoning.slant: Reflects the one-sided nature of the source without adding favoritism.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: No major perspectives present in the article are omitted in the summary text.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Limited to terms like “assertive”; otherwise descriptive.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Uses politically loaded term “illegal immigration”; most other terms are neutral.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced presentation consistent with the source.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No undue deference to elites or dismissal of others.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Replaces loaded phrases (e.g., “avalanche,” “packed arena erupted”) with neutral wording.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: sources_represented lists only the outlet, omitting the Trump/administration perspective explicitly present.
